shownotes: Boogieman Sam contained Daily Blues quotes. The Dripping Tap was dedicated to Cavs' dad who lives in Deniliquin and is an "amazing plumber." Prior to Willoughby’s Beach, which was played for the first time since 2022-04-18 (122 show gap), the band mentions they had to bring their amps from home. Joey mentions his replacement amp makes him sound like he's in The Vines and teased the intro to “Can't Stop” (Red Hot Chili Peppers). Gaia featured a Motor Spirit jam and a drum solo by Cavs. Slow Jam 1 was on the written setlist after Iron Lung and Rattlesnake was on the written setlist after Billabong Valley, but they were not performed.

Following The Dripping Tap, Stu thanked sound tech Gaspard and Joey shouted out Fred Durst of Limp Bizkit, who was also playing at the fest. Danger $$$ was played for the first time since 2021-12-22. (135 show gap) The Reticent Raconteur contained an Alter Me tease. The Lord of Lightning > Cellophane segue was performed for the first time since Desert Days Festival 2018, (220 shows gap). During This Thing, Joey asked for the lights to be put on so he could take a photo for his mom, and the song itself contained an “Incredible” (M Beat) quote.

This show featured the debut of Flamethrower. Joey commented after, that they would do the last half of the song next time. Am I In Heaven? contained Sad Pilot, Flamethrower, and Cut Throat Boogie teases, a Witchcraft tease, and Iron Lung teases and quotes. Following Flamethrower, Joey shouted out opener Grace Cummings. This Thing contained La Grange (ZZ Top) teases and lyrics parodying the song. Iron Lung contained a Hypertension tease. Hypertension contained Witchcraft, Magma, and Ice V teases. Work This Time contained Ice V teases from Cavs. Float Along - Fill Your Lungs contained a metal jam. The written setlist had Doom City and Minimum Brain Size before Float Along - Fill Your Lungs but they were cut for time. Poster by Amy Jean; Edition of 100

Am I In Heaven? contained Sad Pilot, Flamethrower, and Cut Throat Boogie teases, a Witchcraft tease, and Iron Lung teases and quotes. Following Flamethrower, Joey shouted out opener Grace Cummings. This Thing contained La Grange (ZZ Top) teases and lyrics parodying the song. Iron Lung contained a Hypertension tease. Hypertension contained Witchcraft, Magma, and Ice V teases. Work This Time contained Ice V teases from Cavs. Float Along - Fill Your Lungs contained a metal jam. The written setlist had Doom City and Minimum Brain Size before Float Along - Fill Your Lungs but they were cut for time. Poster by Amy Jean; Edition of 100

This show featured the debut of Sad Pilot. Gamma Knife contained a Motor Spirit tease. Mr. Beat contained Sea of Trees and Theia teases. Boogieman Sam contained an East-West (The Butterfield Blues Band) tease. Hot Water featured Ambrose on saxophone and Witchcraft teases. Sea of Trees contained Hot Water and Ice V teases, a Chang'e (Extended) jam with lyrics and Motor Spirit quotes and Ambrose on saxophone. Nuclear Fusion included a fan named Elenie providing the vocal intro. Minimum Brain Size was introduced as a song about Neanderthals. Straws In the Wind contained Daily Blues, Smoke & Mirrors, and Iron Lung quotes. Self-Immolate was dedicated to Jason Galea and featured a drum solo by Cavs. Gaia also featured a drum solo that ended with a “Down Under”(Men at Work) tease by Cavs. Poster by Amy Jean; Edition of 100

This show featured the first full performance of Motor Spirit. Rattlesnake was introduced as a song about snakes and contained Honey and Doom City teases. Honey contained a Sleep Drifter jam. Open Water featured a drum solo by Cavs. Ice V contained Hog Calling Contest teases. Cut Throat Boogie contained Sad Pilot teases and Rats In The Sky, Raw Feel, Field of Vision and Daily Blues quotes. Most Of What I Like contained a “Praise You” (Fatboy Slim) jam with Flight b741 teases. Following Most Of What I Like, Stu teased “Sweet Home Alabama” (Lynyrd Skynyrd). The Lord of Lightning was introduced as a song about shots by Stu, and about when Stu got struck by lightning by Ambrose, and contained Cut Throat Boogie and I'm In Your Mind teases. The Alter Me I Jam contained a Hypertension jam with lyrics and Witchcraft and Cellophane teases. I'm Not In Your Mind contained Robot Stop teases. Cellophane was also introduced as a song about shots by Stu and contained Motor Spirit quotes. Joey shouted out opener Grace Cummings after I'm In Your Mind Fuzz. Dragon was introduced as a song about dragons. Poster by Amy Jean; Edition of 100

jamchart_notes: After a smooth transition out of Ice V, Amby’s harmonica wail throws us into the Cut Throat Boogie. The band plays the first couple of verses straight, which is a stomping good time as always, but the real kicker is the appended blues jam. Amby once again takes the lead with a killer harmonica solo, before the band starts dishing out a dynamic plethora of teases of Flight b741 material, including repeated Sad Pilot riffs, as well as the never-before heard Rats in the Sky, Raw Feel and Field of Vision.

This show featured the debut of Le Risque, which featured vocals from Cavs, making it his vocal debut. Grace Cummings unfortunately had to cancel at short notice, and was replaced with local Austrian band, Hypnotic Floor. Venusian 2 was introduced as a song about Venus. Slow Jam 1 contained Ice V, Sad Pilot, and Loyalty teases and a Chang'e (Extended) jam with lyrics. O.N.E. contained a Straws In The Wind intro. Billabong Valley was performed without Ambrose on guitar, but Stu on guitar for the second half and contained a Minimum Brain Size and Doom City tease. The banter between Billabong Valley and Le Risqué contained quotes and teases of My Generation by Limp Bizkit. Garden Goblin was introduced as being called Borat after Cookie’s vocals on the song. Iron Lung was dedicated to John Frusciante and contained Murder of The Universe, Witchcraft, and Hypertension teases. Hypertension contained a Robot Stop intro and teases of the new song and Evil Death Roll. Evil Death Roll did not contain the Invisible face segment and had Witchcraft, Converge, and Crumbling Castle teases. After the show, Ambrose sang “so long, farewell” from The sound of Music Poster by Amy Jean; Edition of 100

jamchart_notes: Opening a set in Berlin, the band starts with an unfamiliar setup of a large synth setup in the middle of the stage. Their playing slowly lets it become more and more obvious what they're going to debut, with low synths and samples strewn of Stu singing strewn throughout. It quickly blossoms into an excellent (and the first!) version of Extinction in a live setting, with yet more samples and synths not seen in Studio. It then slows into the bassline of Gondii, kicking off a two-in-one synth debut special.
shownotes: This show featured a new synth table setup and the debuts of Extinction and Gondii. Grace Cummings unfortunately missed a second show due to illness, and was replaced with local Berlin artist Cosey Mueller. Extinction contained a Gondii tease and Flamethrower quotes. Gondii contained Extinction teases and quotes. The Dripping Tap contained Sad Pilot teases, Motor Spirit quotes, and a Witchcraft tease. Only Parts I, II, and III of The River were performed and they contained a Motor Spirit tease, Wah Wah teases and quotes, and Extinction teases. Wah Wah contained The River teases and Extinction teases and quotes. Let Me Mend The Past contained an It Ain't Me, Babe (Bob Dylan) quote. Before Gila Monster, the band thanks crew members Nico and Laura for building the new synth table setup at which point Cookie teases Happy Birthday. Gila Monster contained a Dragon tease. All Is Known and Rattlesnake were on the physical setlist (before and after Doom City respectively), but were cut for time. Poster by Amy Jean; Edition of 100

Only Parts I, II, and III of The River were performed and they contained a Motor Spirit tease, Wah Wah teases and quotes, and Extinction teases. Wah Wah contained The River teases and Extinction teases and quotes. Let Me Mend The Past contained an It Ain't Me, Babe (Bob Dylan) quote. Before Gila Monster, the band thanks crew members Nico and Laura for building the new synth table setup at which point Cookie teases Happy Birthday. Gila Monster contained a Dragon tease. All Is Known and Rattlesnake were on the physical setlist (before and after Doom City respectively), but were cut for time. Poster by Amy Jean; Edition of 100

This show featured the debut of The Silver Cord. Float Along - Fill Your Lungs contained a Motor Spirit jam with lyrics. Mr. Beat contained a Hot Water tease, Motor Spirit quotes, and a Theia (Extended) jam with lyrics. Evil Death Roll contained a Theia tease and Witchcraft teases. Iron Lung contained Hot Water and Stairway to Heaven (Led Zeppelin) teases. Hot Water contained teases of “Third Stone From The Sun” (Jimi Hendrix), Gondii and Flamethrower. Hypertension contained Work This Time teases. Gaia featured a drum solo by Cavs and an extended full band jam following the drums. Inner Cell, Loyalty, Horology, and This Thing were on the physical setlist after The Silver Cord but were cut. Poster by Amy Jean; Edition of 150

shownotes: This show featured the debut of Daily Blues. Grace Cummings unfortunately missed a third show due to illness, and was replaced with Naarm synth-punk band Dr Sure's Unusual Practice. Crumbling Castle contained a Witchcraft tease. The Fourth Colour contained a Daily Blues jam with lyrics. Rattlesnake contained an intro jam while Ambrose ran offstage. The jam contained Sleep Drifter, Honey, and Straws In The Wind teases and the song itself contained teases of Honey, “Whole Lotta Rosie” (AC/DC) and Sleep Drifter. Wah Wah contained a The River intro. Only Part I, II, and III of The River were performed and they contained a Sad Pilot tease and Wah Wah teases and quotes. The Bitter Boogie was played with the drum pattern from Muddy Water. Poster by Amy Jean; Edition of 150

Crumbling Castle contained a Witchcraft tease. The Fourth Colour contained a Daily Blues jam with lyrics. Rattlesnake contained an intro jam while Ambrose ran offstage. The jam contained Sleep Drifter, Honey, and Straws In The Wind teases and the song itself contained teases of Honey, “Whole Lotta Rosie” (AC/DC) and Sleep Drifter. Wah Wah contained a The River intro. Only Part I, II, and III of The River were performed and they contained a Sad Pilot tease and Wah Wah teases and quotes. The Bitter Boogie was played with the drum pattern from Muddy Water. Poster by Amy Jean; Edition of 150

Ambrose joined fellow Wide Awake act C.O.F.F.I.N. on harmonica during their set. Cheryl Waters (of KEXP) introduced the band as they came on stage. This Thing contained an “Incredible” (M Beat) quote. Evil Death Roll contained Motor Spirit and The Silver Cord quotes. Head On/Pill [1] contained a Hypertension jam with lyrics. Head On/Pill [2] contained Chang'e (Extended) and Witchcraft jams with lyrics, Sea of Trees, Witchcraft, Motor Spirit, and Am I In Heaven? teases and quotes.

This show featured the debut of Raw Feel. Grace Cummings unfortunately missed a fourth show due to illness, and was replaced with Atlanta, Georgia psych-punk band Upchuck, who Joey thanked at the beginning of the show. Gamma Knife contained an intro jam and Motor Spirit teases. The Dripping Tap, which contained a Head On/Pill tease, had Stu's amp break in the middle, which lead to the song being paused for a short time during which Joey teased “Hey Joe” (Jimi Hendrix) followed by a quote from Ambrose. Nuclear Fusion featured audience member, Earl, on vocals. Honey contained a Sleep Drifter intro that contained Nuclear Fusion teases, and the song itself contained Billabong Valley, Straws In The Wind, and O.N.E teases. Straws In The Wind contained a chant of 'Free Palestine'. Venusian 2 was on the printed setlist after Perihelion but was not played. Poster by Amy Jean; Edition of 150

Grace Cummings unfortunately missed a fifth show due to illness, and was replaced with Australian rock band C.O.F.F.I.N., who were joined by Ambrose during their opening set. The Lord of Lightning contained Cellophane and Witchcraft teases. I'm Not In Your Mind contained Am I In Heaven? teases and Daily Blues teases and quotes. The Bitter Boogie contained the drums from Muddy Water, Head On/Pill teases, Rats In The Sky, Hog Calling Contest, Antarctica, and Daily Blues quotes. Cellophane contained Rattlesnake teases. Ben Portnoy (drummer of C.O.F.F.I.N) joined the band for Rattlesnake, which contained Automation, Honey, Billabong Valley, and Minimum Brain Size teases, Honey teases and quotes with the lyrics changed to "Mad Dog tastes like honey", Daily Blues, Boogieman Sam, Oddlife, and Straws In The Wind quotes, and an Oddlife and Billabong Valley jam with lyrics. Poster by Amy Jean; Edition of 150

Ben Portnoy (drummer of C.O.F.F.I.N) joined the band for Rattlesnake, which contained Automation, Honey, Billabong Valley, and Minimum Brain Size teases, Honey teases and quotes with the lyrics changed to "Mad Dog tastes like honey", Daily Blues, Boogieman Sam, Oddlife, and Straws In The Wind quotes, and an Oddlife and Billabong Valley jam with lyrics. Poster by Amy Jean; Edition of 150

Grace Cummings returned from illness as the opening act. Float Along - Fill Your Lungs contained a Dragon jam with lyrics. Boogieman Sam contained a Sad Pilot jam with lyrics, "Midnight Rambler" (The Rolling Stones) quotes, and “Boogie” (Carson) teases and quotes. The Dripping Tap was dedicated to "everyone with diarrhea" and contained a You Can Be Your Sillouhette jam and Mycelium quotes. The Wheel was dedicated to David, the band's bus driver. This Thing contained an “Incredible” (M Beat) quote. Self-Immolate contained a drum solo. Following Converge, Joey shouted out opener Grace Cummings. Witchcraft was on the printed setlist after Converge, but was skipped. Poster by Amy Jean; Edition of 100

Gondii contained Extinction quotes and The Grim Reaper quotes and teases. A segue into The Grim Reaper out of Gondii was attempted but scrapped, and led to The Grim Reaper being skipped. Before Evil Death Roll, Stu teased Voodoo Child (Slight Return) and Little Wing (both by Jimi Hendrix). Joey yelled Free Palestine in Pleura. Poster by Amy Jean; Edition of 100

This show featured the (incomplete) debut of Mirage City, and the debuts of Time = $$$ and You Can Be Your Silhouette. Iron Lung contained Witchcraft teases. Most Of What I Like contained Praise You (Fatboy Slim) teases. Time = $$$ was introduced as “Stairway to Heaven” (Led Zeppelin), which was then teased. You Can Be Your Silhouette contained an Iron Lung tease. The Bitter Boogie contained the drum pattern from Muddy Water and a lyric change to "shits his pants". Hot Water contained a Cellophane tease. Following Hot Water, Joey noted that the band has a microtonal acoustic guitar, but only one, and jokingly suggested that Stu should play Rattlesnake solo, who then sang it along with teases of it from Joey and Ambrose. Soon after, Stu teased “Hey Joe” (Jimi Hendrix) which led to Joey remarking he used to get made fun of for playing it in school. Following Trapdoor, Joey called Cavs down to ask him when he played drums, to which he replied around 16. Ambrose then quoted Pleura. Sad Pilot was introduced as a song about being sad. Following Sad Pilot, Stu quoted Rattlesnake again. Sense contained You Can Be Your Sillouhette teases and a Witchcraft tease. This Thing contained a Le Risque intro and an "Incredible" (M Beat) quote. The intro to Slow Jam 1 contained a The River tease and the song itself contained Ice V teases. Before Witchcraft, Stu told a story about a fan giving him a CD of acoustic songs from PetroDragonic Apocalypse and that inspired the band to play Witchcraft acoustic. Poster by Amy Jean; Edition of 100

showtitle: Drag Night at Les Nuits De Fourviere
shownotes: The band dressed in drag for the 2nd ever drag night, exactly 1 year after the show on 2023-06-03. This show featured the debut of Theia. Theia contained Motor Spirit and Extinction quotes. Extinction contained The Grim Reaper teases. The Grim Reaper contained Get Low (Lil Jon & The East Side Boyz), Work It (Missy Elliot), Torture Chamber, and Body Movin' and Intergalactic (Beastie Boys) quotes. Following The Grim Reaper, Joey wished everyone a happy pride month. Gaia contained a drum solo over a metal vamp from the band. After Gaia, Joey shouted out Amy Taylor of Amyl and the Sniffers for doing his makeup pre-show. Ambrose sung a verse of Witchcraft for Stu due to Stu's pedal breaking. Cellophane contained Magma teases and quotes. Poster by Amy Jean; Edition of 100

After Gaia, Joey shouted out Amy Taylor of Amyl and the Sniffers for doing his makeup pre-show. Ambrose sung a verse of Witchcraft for Stu due to Stu's pedal breaking. Cellophane contained Magma teases and quotes. Poster by Amy Jean; Edition of 100

The entire band sat in with Grace Cummings for her song "Heaven". Joey thanked Grace before the show started, and Stu mentioned that they let the crew write the setlist. Before Danger $$$, Stu mentioned Gaspard requested the song. Wah Wah contained a The River intro. Only Part I, II, and III of The River was performed and they contained Wah Wah quotes with the "wah wah" refrain changed to "boogie boogie". Road Train was requested by Andrea. Mirage City was played for Jason Galea and was played electric for the first time. Before Sad Pilot, they dedicated the song to Sammy and talked about him recording and playing pedal steel on the new album. Work This Time was dedicated to Grace Cummings. The Dripping Tap was played for Maclay Heriot and contained a Motor Spirit jam, The Silver Cord teases, and Sea of Trees teases. The intro jam to Sleep Drifter contained O.N.E, Sleep Drifter, and Honey teases. Honey contained O.N.E, If Not Now, Then When?, and Billabong Valley teases. Ambrose quoted Ain’t No Mountain High Enough (Marvin Gaye & Tammi Terrell) before Billabong Valley. Before Motor Spirit, Stu asked the crowd who was throwing dinosaurs on stage, and Ambrose quoted Deserted Dunes Welcome Weary Feet. Motor Spirit was played for Jess Lewis because "it sounds like Tool". After Dragon, the band discussed playing Am I In Heaven? but pivoted to Self-Immolate.

jamchart_notes: A masterclass in teasing and a very classically gizz approach to The Dripping Tap. A blissful jam opens up after the second chorus, with Antarctica teases and quotes driving the jam towards a rewarding peak. One of the greatest ways to open a tour.
shownotes:
This show featured the debut of Antarctica. The Dripping Tap contained Chang'e teases, You Can Be Your Silhouette teases, Antarctica teases & quotes, and Am I In Heaven? teases. Evil Death Roll was introduced as a song about Bob Dylan and contained Witchcraft and Robot Stop teases. Ice V contained a Witchcraft tease and a "Blindness" (The Fall) jam. The Fourth Colour, which was incomplete, contained a Daily Blues jam with lyrics and "Whole Lotta Rosie" (AC/DC) teases. Supercell was introduced as a song about orange juice. Following Motor Spirit, Joey introduced the band members. Gaia contained a jam with Joey on bass, which contained a Motor Spirit tease and a drum solo.

Poster by Jason Galea
Standard Edition of 650
Holographic Edition of 200

Evil Death Roll was introduced as a song about Bob Dylan and contained Witchcraft and Robot Stop teases. Ice V contained a Witchcraft tease and a "Blindness" (The Fall) jam. The Fourth Colour, which was incomplete, contained a Daily Blues jam with lyrics and "Whole Lotta Rosie" (AC/DC) teases. Supercell was introduced as a song about orange juice. Following Motor Spirit, Joey introduced the band members. Gaia contained a jam with Joey on bass, which contained a Motor Spirit tease and a drum solo.

Poster by Jason Galea
Standard Edition of 650

jamchart_notes: Cuts off after verse 1 and a slow-building blues jam reminiscent of Daily Blues starts. Eventually changes to a regular Gizz rock jam, then starts alternating quickly between blues jam and rock jam, amping up and culminating in some metal flourishes. After a few more quick and somewhat awkward alternations and a false ending, the jam ends, leaving the song unfinished.

jamchart_notes: The drum solo and bass accompaniment from Joey quickly break from norms. This drum solo is soon transformed into a haunting and spacey jam that builds steadily towards the outro and an epic close to the show.

This show featured the debut of Field of Vision. Daily Blues was preceeded by a Rock and Roll (Led Zeppelin) tease. Gamma Knife contained a Motor Spirit tease. Boogieman Sam contained The Bitter Boogie and Work This Time teases, a Hog Calling Contest vocal jam, and Rats In The Sky quotes. I'm Not In Your Mind contained You Can Be Your Silhouette and Crumbling Castle teases. Cellophane contained Altered Beast teases. Before This Thing, which was introduced as a song about Ambrose and had alternate "New York City" lyrics, Converge was teased. After This Thing, Joey and Stu shouted out "Raygun", the Australian Olypmic breakdancer. Self-Immolate was introduced as a song about God. Straws In The Wind was dedicated to Alex Harvey.  Doom City contained an intro jam. Nuclear Fusion was not on the the printed setlist and featured an audience member (Gabby) on vocals. Rattlesnake contained Honey, Nuclear Fusion, and Oddlife teases, a Field of Vision quote, and Sleep Drifter teases and quotes.

Poster by Jason Galea
Standard Edition of 600
Holographic Edition of 150

After This Thing, Joey and Stu shouted out "Raygun", the Australian Olypmic breakdancer. Self-Immolate was introduced as a song about God. Straws In The Wind was dedicated to Alex Harvey.  Doom City contained an intro jam. Nuclear Fusion was not on the the printed setlist and featured an audience member (Gabby) on vocals. Rattlesnake contained Honey, Nuclear Fusion, and Oddlife teases, a Field of Vision quote, and Sleep Drifter teases and quotes.

Poster by Jason Galea
Standard Edition of 600
Holographic Edition of 150

Holographic Edition of 150

Hot Water contained Robot Stop, Am I In Heaven?, and The Dripping Tap teases. Robot Stop, which was incomplete, contained Ice V, Daily Blues, and Am I In Heaven? teases and Field of Vision quotes. The second Hot Water contained a chant of "New York City". Ice V contained a "Blindness" (The Fall) jam and improvised lyrics. The Bitter Boogie contained the drums from Muddy Water and Ice V teases. You Can Be Your Silhouette was introduced as a song about being who you want to be. Hot Wax contained You Can Be Your Silhouette teases. Converge was introduced as a song about storms before Planet B, as Stu confused them on the setlist. The Great Chain of Being contained an "On The Road Again" (Canned Heat) tease. Motor Spirit was dedicated to Cavs "because it's about cars" and contained a You Can Be Your Silhouette and Robot Stop tease. Slow Jam 1 contained Crumbling Castle, Sad Pilot and Ice V teases. Billabong Valley was preceded by a "Microtonal Jazz Jam" with Stu referencing Sexy Alien and Joey singing The Star Spangled Banner. In the song itself, Ambrose crowdsurfed on an inflatable gator and quoted Should I Stay Or Should I Go (The Clash). Following Automation, Joey shouted out opener Geese. A crowd member then told the band to play Fishing For Fishies and the full band (besides Cavs) teased it. The band then started to name classic rock songs and teased Stairway To Heaven and Moby Dick (both Led Zeppelin), and Smoke On the Water (Deep Purple). Stu also shouted out Australian band Cold Chisel. Han-Tyumi, The Confused Cyborg contained a Hypertension tease. Am I In Heaven? contained Evil Death Roll, and Converge teases and Hot Water teases and quotes. Sad Pilot was on the printed setlist but was skipped.

Poster by Jason Galea
Standard Edition of 1200
Holographic Edition of 300

Motor Spirit was dedicated to Cavs "because it's about cars" and contained a You Can Be Your Silhouette and Robot Stop tease. Slow Jam 1 contained Crumbling Castle, Sad Pilot and Ice V teases. Billabong Valley was preceded by a "Microtonal Jazz Jam" with Stu referencing Sexy Alien and Joey singing The Star Spangled Banner. In the song itself, Ambrose crowdsurfed on an inflatable gator and quoted Should I Stay Or Should I Go (The Clash). Following Automation, Joey shouted out opener Geese. A crowd member then told the band to play Fishing For Fishies and the full band (besides Cavs) teased it. The band then started to name classic rock songs and teased Stairway To Heaven and Moby Dick (both Led Zeppelin), and Smoke On the Water (Deep Purple). Stu also shouted out Australian band Cold Chisel. Han-Tyumi, The Confused Cyborg contained a Hypertension tease. Am I In Heaven? contained Evil Death Roll, and Converge teases and Hot Water teases and quotes. Sad Pilot was on the printed setlist but was skipped.

Poster by Jason Galea
Standard Edition of 1200
Holographic Edition of 300

jamchart_notes: Performing the first verse from an inflatable gator raft held up by the crowd, Ambrose delivers impassioned vocals throughout the song. Colorful guitar work winds into Billabong’s iconic slow-down section where, after Stu takes a quick restroom break, the band stick the landing. The first of the “Amby wander” Billabong Valley’s that became a definitive staple of the 2024 NA tour.

This show featured the debut of Flight b741. Extinction contained The Grim Reaper quotes. The Grim Reaper contained a Gondii tease and Torture Chamber quotes. Perihelion featured an audience member named Timmy on Joey's guitar, and was dedicated to his friend Joey who sadly passed in an accident earlier in the year. Magma was dedicated by Joey to John Bonham and contained a You Can Be Your Silhouette tease. After Daily Blues, Joey shouted out Lucas. Wah Wah contained a The River intro and jam. Hypertension contained Ice V and Work This Time teases. Float Along - Fill Your Lungs contained a Motor Spirit and Dragon tease. Perihelion was not on the printed setlist, but Sea of Trees was (which was skipped).

Poster by Jason Galea
Standard Edition of 600
Holographic Edition of 200

footnote: With Timmy; Dedicated to his friend Joey
footnotes: ["With Timmy; Dedicated to his friend Joey"]
jamchart_notes: The band was joined on stage by audience member Timmy, who played Joey's part on the song. The emotional rendition of the song was dedicated to Timmy's late friend Joey.

jamchart_notes: A confident and ripping performance of Magma. Tight and creative jams that highlight the bands guitar work are matched with some of Ambrose’s more inspired saxophone playing. The end is taken to unusual lengths as the band cools the signature double time jam down before launching into the one of the heaviest outros Magma has ever seen.

This show featured the debut of Flight b741. Extinction contained The Grim Reaper quotes. The Grim Reaper contained a Gondii tease and Torture Chamber quotes. Perihelion featured an audience member named Timmy on Joey's guitar, and was dedicated to his friend Joey who sadly passed in an accident earlier in the year. Magma was dedicated by Joey to John Bonham and contained a You Can Be Your Silhouette tease. After Daily Blues, Joey shouted out Lucas. Wah Wah contained a The River intro and jam. Hypertension contained Ice V and Work This Time teases. Float Along - Fill Your Lungs contained a Motor Spirit and Dragon tease. Perihelion was not on the printed setlist, but Sea of Trees was (which was skipped).

This show featured the debut of Rats In The Sky. Mars For the Rich was dedicated to Joey's friend Laura from high school who moved to Portland, Maine. This Thing contained an Incredible (M Beat) quote. I'm Not In Your Mind contained Witchcraft teases. Following Le Risque, Joey said that the song should be in Happy Gilmore 2. Let Me Mend The Past contained an It Ain't Me, Babe (Bob Dylan) quote. Following Let Me Mend The Past, Ambrose quoted I'd Rather Go Blind (Etta James). Before The Dripping Tap, Joey shouted out opener Geese. The song itself, during which Stu was crowdsurfed to the nearby river and back, was dedicated to Bill Cavanagh and contained two Antarctica vocal jams with some of the lyrics changed to "Portland, Maine" and to being about Stu swimming in the lake nearby (some of The Dripping Tap lyrics were changed to this as well) and Sea of Trees, Head On/Pill, Witchcraft, and Alter Me teases, a Surfin' Safari (The Beach Boys) quote, as well as Joey teasing Forty Six & 2 (Tool) for a large portion of the song. Venusian 2 and Sea of Trees were on the printed setlist (before Mars For the Rich and The Dripping Tap respectively) but were not played. Poster by Jason Galea Standard Edition - 650 Foil Edition- 250

jamchart_notes: Early rendition of the song featuring heavy trance feel followed by a strong build up to the climax.

jamchart_notes: river motor spirit and tsc jams, various quotes
shownotes:
Before Theia, Ambrose quoted Fried. The song itself contained Motor Spirit quotes, a The River jam with Wah Wah quotes, and Motor Spirit and The Silver Cord jams with lyrics. Joey did Michael Jackson-style vocal improv in the intro to Ambergris, which was introduced as a song about sperm whales and contained a short drum solo. Joey did an impression of Guy Fieri before Rats In The Sky. Cavs teased Rock and Roll (Led Zeppelin) before Sad Pilot. After Rats in the Sky, the band heckled Jason Galea to join on tambourine. Before Flight b741, the band got Cavs to hit the gong on his kit. After Flight b741, the band explained that Geelong, aka G-troit, is the sister city to Detroit, as both had a Ford production factory, which closed and destroyed the town. Joey teased Purple Haze (Jimi Hendrix) and Cavs teased Rock and Roll again. You Can Be Your Silhouette contained an extended intro where the band talked about how their talkback mics work and jazz notes. During the song itself, which contained a The End (The Doors) quote, it was described as a song about being yourself. During the intro to Straws In The Wind, the band joked about Ambrose swimming over to Canada and Ambrose walked up to the lights and sound booth.  Nuclear Fusion featured Vincent, a kid from the audience on the intro. Following the intro, Joey teased Twist (Korn). After Nuclear Fusion, Stu shouted out the stream crew. Honey contained O.N.E teases. Minimum Brain Size was introduced as a song about "loser hypermasculine people."  Prior to Rattlesnake Joey conducted a Q&A session with crowd members and Sam. Prior to Billabong Valley, Cavs ran offstage. During Billabong Valley, Joey said he can do a Limp Bizkit beat cause he doesn’t have an electric guitar and doesn’t want to take it to the [Dave] Matthews Bridge, followed by Ambrose climbing on top of the equipment truck on the side of the venue, leading to the line “I’m on the truck!” Her and I (Slow Jam 2), which was last played 2023-06-18 (50 show gap), contained a Slow Jam 1 intro as well as Iron Lung teases.

Poster by Jason Galea
Standard Edition - 600
Foil Edition- 200

Before Theia, Ambrose quoted Fried. The song itself contained Motor Spirit quotes, a The River jam with Wah Wah quotes, and Motor Spirit and The Silver Cord jams with lyrics. Joey did Michael Jackson-style vocal improv in the intro to Ambergris, which was introduced as a song about sperm whales and contained a short drum solo. Joey did an impression of Guy Fieri before Rats In The Sky. Cavs teased Rock and Roll (Led Zeppelin) before Sad Pilot. After Rats in the Sky, the band heckled Jason Galea to join on tambourine. Before Flight b741, the band got Cavs to hit the gong on his kit. After Flight b741, the band explained that Geelong, aka G-troit, is the sister city to Detroit, as both had a Ford production factory, which closed and destroyed the town. Joey teased Purple Haze (Jimi Hendrix) and Cavs teased Rock and Roll again. You Can Be Your Silhouette contained an extended intro where the band talked about how their talkback mics work and jazz notes. During the song itself, which contained a The End (The Doors) quote, it was described as a song about being yourself. During the intro to Straws In The Wind, the band joked about Ambrose swimming over to Canada and Ambrose walked up to the lights and sound booth.  Nuclear Fusion featured Vincent, a kid from the audience on the intro. Following the intro, Joey teased Twist (Korn). After Nuclear Fusion, Stu shouted out the stream crew. Honey contained O.N.E teases. Minimum Brain Size was introduced as a song about "loser hypermasculine people."  Prior to Rattlesnake Joey conducted a Q&A session with crowd members and Sam. Prior to Billabong Valley, Cavs ran offstage. During Billabong Valley, Joey said he can do a Limp Bizkit beat cause he doesn’t have an electric guitar and doesn’t want to take it to the [Dave] Matthews Bridge, followed by Ambrose climbing on top of the equipment truck on the side of the venue, leading to the line “I’m on the truck!” Her and I (Slow Jam 2), which was last played 2023-06-18 (50 show gap), contained a Slow Jam 1 intro as well as Iron Lung teases.

Poster by Jason Galea
Standard Edition - 600
Foil Edition- 200

Before Theia, Ambrose quoted Fried. The song itself contained Motor Spirit quotes, a The River jam with Wah Wah quotes, and Motor Spirit and The Silver Cord jams with lyrics. Joey did Michael Jackson-style vocal improv in the intro to Ambergris, which was introduced as a song about sperm whales and contained a short drum solo. Joey did an impression of Guy Fieri before Rats In The Sky. Cavs teased Rock and Roll (Led Zeppelin) before Sad Pilot. After Rats in the Sky, the band heckled Jason Galea to join on tambourine. Before Flight b741, the band got Cavs to hit the gong on his kit. After Flight b741, the band explained that Geelong, aka G-troit, is the sister city to Detroit, as both had a Ford production factory, which closed and destroyed the town. Joey teased Purple Haze (Jimi Hendrix) and Cavs teased Rock and Roll again. You Can Be Your Silhouette contained an extended intro where the band talked about how their talkback mics work and jazz notes. During the song itself, which contained a The End (The Doors) quote, it was described as a song about being yourself. During the intro to Straws In The Wind, the band joked about Ambrose swimming over to Canada and Ambrose walked up to the lights and sound booth.  Nuclear Fusion featured Vincent, a kid from the audience on the intro. Following the intro, Joey teased Twist (Korn). After Nuclear Fusion, Stu shouted out the stream crew. Honey contained O.N.E teases. Minimum Brain Size was introduced as a song about "loser hypermasculine people."  Prior to Rattlesnake Joey conducted a Q&A session with crowd members and Sam. Prior to Billabong Valley, Cavs ran offstage. During Billabong Valley, Joey said he can do a Limp Bizkit beat cause he doesn’t have an electric guitar and doesn’t want to take it to the [Dave] Matthews Bridge, followed by Ambrose climbing on top of the equipment truck on the side of the venue, leading to the line “I’m on the truck!” Her and I (Slow Jam 2), which was last played 2023-06-18 (50 show gap), contained a Slow Jam 1 intro as well as Iron Lung teases.

Poster by Jason Galea
Standard Edition - 600
Foil Edition- 200

Before Theia, Ambrose quoted Fried. The song itself contained Motor Spirit quotes, a The River jam with Wah Wah quotes, and Motor Spirit and The Silver Cord jams with lyrics. Joey did Michael Jackson-style vocal improv in the intro to Ambergris, which was introduced as a song about sperm whales and contained a short drum solo. Joey did an impression of Guy Fieri before Rats In The Sky. Cavs teased Rock and Roll (Led Zeppelin) before Sad Pilot. After Rats in the Sky, the band heckled Jason Galea to join on tambourine. Before Flight b741, the band got Cavs to hit the gong on his kit. After Flight b741, the band explained that Geelong, aka G-troit, is the sister city to Detroit, as both had a Ford production factory, which closed and destroyed the town. Joey teased Purple Haze (Jimi Hendrix) and Cavs teased Rock and Roll again. Before Magma, Joey thanked Geese for opening. Muddy Water contained the drums from The Bitter Boogie along with teases, and featured Ambrose on sax. Raw Feel contained a Sympathy For the Devil (The Rolling Stones) tease. Ice V featured Jimi Hendrix-style scat vocals from Joey. Joey introduced Le Risque as a song about taking risks. Before Superbug, Joey told Stu not to write another song like it because he wrote it and it (Covid) happened. Superbug was played for the first time since 2023-06-07 (59 show gap). Before Dragon, Killing in the Name (Rage Against the Machine) was teased. Dragon was not on the printed setlist but was played.

This show featured the debut of Gilgamesh. After Doom City, Ambrose shouted out opener Geese. East West Link was played for the first time since 2023-03-16 (78 show gap). Cavs teased Rock and Roll (Led Zeppelin) before Daily Blues and Stu said, "This song is about loving your enemy." Ambrose changed lyrics in Daily Blues to shout of the city of Philadelphia. Hot Water contained Antarctica, Float Along - Fill Your Lungs, and Grow Wings and Fly... quotes, Head On/Pill teases, and Daily Blues teases and quotes. Le Risque was introduced as "a little bit of ZZ Top" and followed an education by the band on Australian legend John Farnham. The River contained Gilgamesh quotes and Ice V teases. I'm Not In Your Mind contained a Motor Spirit and a The Land Before Timeland tease. Cellophane contained The Land Before Timeland teases. Gilgamesh contained a Set tease. Set contained Ice V teases and "I love you" lyrics from Stu. After the show, Ambrose said that night he was using a cheesesteak for a pillow. Poster by Jason Galea Standard Edition - 600 Foil Edition - 200

Theia contained The Silver Cord quotes and Motor Spirit teases. Ambrose referenced "four fried chickens, and a Coke" from The Blues Brothers because he was eating chicken and quoted Rock Around the Clock (Bill Haley & His Comets) before Evil Death Roll. The song itself contained Theia quotes. Muddy Water contained the drums from The Bitter Boogie as well as quotes. The Bitter Boogie contained the drums from Muddy Water, quotes of Field of Vision with the lyrics changed to "Field of Chicken", altered lyrics to reference chicken, and a Muddy Water outro. Raw Feel was introduced as a song about the feeling of putting your hand into a bag of fried chicken. Cavs teased Rock and Roll and Moby Dick (Led Zeppelin) before Field of Vision, which had its lyrics changed to Field of Chicken. Hell was introduced as a song about planet Earth. Hot Wax was on the printed setlist before Hell, but was skipped. Shook Ones, Part II (Mobb Deep) was referenced by Ambrose before Head On/Pill. Head On/Pill contained The Dripping Tap and Chang'e teases and featured Ambrose on sax.

Poster by Jason Galea
Standard Edition - 500
Foil Edition - 150

jamchart_notes: Transitioned out of Extinction, the band drifts from the song’s electronic DNA making way for an electric rendition that prominently features guitar. The song’s bright synths lead to delicate guitar lines before a commanding jam climbs to an overdrive soaked crescendo.

The Bitter Boogie contained the drums from Muddy Water, quotes of Field of Vision with the lyrics changed to "Field of Chicken", altered lyrics to reference chicken, and a Muddy Water outro. Raw Feel was introduced as a song about the feeling of putting your hand into a bag of fried chicken. Cavs teased Rock and Roll and Moby Dick (Led Zeppelin) before Field of Vision, which had its lyrics changed to Field of Chicken. Hell was introduced as a song about planet Earth. Hot Wax was on the printed setlist before Hell, but was skipped. Shook Ones, Part II (Mobb Deep) was referenced by Ambrose before Head On/Pill. Head On/Pill contained The Dripping Tap and Chang'e teases and featured Ambrose on sax.

Poster by Jason Galea
Standard Edition - 500
Foil Edition - 150

Hypertension contained Boogieman Sam teases and quotes and Ice V teases. Ice V contained Hypertension and Blindness (The Fall) teases. Antarctica contained an outro jam without drums for Cavs to take a piss break. Before Gaia, Stu joked about how Supercell sounds like "soup for sale". Gaia contained a drum solo during which fans gave shirts to Stu. Before Mars For the Rich Stu put a hat (that he had earlier picked up from the stage) on backwards, jokingly compared himself to Lleyton Hewitt (an Australian former tennis player who was known for wearing hats backwards), and imitated his trademark fist pump. Swan Song (#2) contained Dragon quotes. Float Along - Fill Your Lungs contained a Dragon jam with lyrics.

Poster by Jason Galea

Evil Death Roll contained You Can Be Your Silhouette and Field of Vision teases. Field of Vision was dedicated to the city of Nashville. Raw Feel contained a Witchcraft tease. Black Tooth was played for the first time since 2022-10-02. (118 show gap) Le Risque was dedicated to Johnny Cash. I'm Not In Your Mind featured Ambrose on sax. Hot Wax contained a Am I In Heaven? tease. After I'm In Your Mind Fuzz, Stu thanked Trey [Anastasio] because a fan threw a shirt on stage of the Phish logo with the letters altered to say Gizz. Slow Jam 1 featured Ambrose on sax and Hypertension, You Can Be Your Sillouhette, and Witchcraft teases. Perihelion featured Jay Weinberg (formerly of Slipknot) on drums. Chang'e contained an intro jam with Extinction quotes and the song itself contained Witchcraft quotes. Extinction contained an Intergalactic (Beastie Boys) quote.

Poster by Jason Galea
Standard Edition - 650
Foil Edition - 200

Black Tooth was played for the first time since 2022-10-02. (118 show gap) Le Risque was dedicated to Johnny Cash. I'm Not In Your Mind featured Ambrose on sax. Hot Wax contained a Am I In Heaven? tease. After I'm In Your Mind Fuzz, Stu thanked Trey [Anastasio] because a fan threw a shirt on stage of the Phish logo with the letters altered to say Gizz. Slow Jam 1 featured Ambrose on sax and Hypertension, You Can Be Your Sillouhette, and Witchcraft teases. Perihelion featured Jay Weinberg (formerly of Slipknot) on drums. Chang'e contained an intro jam with Extinction quotes and the song itself contained Witchcraft quotes. Extinction contained an Intergalactic (Beastie Boys) quote.

Poster by Jason Galea

Robot Stop featured Ambrose on saxophone and contained Sad Pilot, Converge, and Witchcraft teases. Gamma Knife contained a Motor Spirit tease. People-Vultures contained Iron Lung teases & quotes. Mr. Beat contained Sad Pilot teases. Sad Pilot was introduced as a song about donkeys and dedicated to band soundman Joe Santarpia.  Rats in the Sky was introduced as a song about chickens in the sky. Cut Throat Boogie contained The Bitter Boogie teases and was dedicated to Magic Sam. You Can Be Your Silhouette was dedicated to Alex Brettin of Mild High Club, who then briefly appeared on stage. The Fourth Colour contained quotes of Gila Monster and Mars For the Rich. Straws In the Wind contained a Rattlesnake quote. Welcome to An Altered Future was played for the first time since 2018-03-16. (304 show gap) This was also the first time the suite was performed in full. Set featured Joey on guitar and Ambrose on saxophone. Head On/Pill was not on the written setlist and featured Ambrose on saxophone and contained Am I In Heaven? and Robot Stop teases, Chang'e teases and quotes, and a Hot Water jam with lyrics. At the end of the show, Ambrose thanked Chicago for The Blues Brothers and blues music.
venuename: Huntington Bank Pavilion at Northerly Island

jamchart_notes: Cuts off after verse 1 and goes to a creative blues jam, similar to 8/15, but a bit more jammy and explorative and less straightforward. They jam and quote Gila Monster and eventually Mars For the Rich. The jam quiets down and leads to a full stop, then they finish the song post-jam.

You Can Be Your Silhouette was dedicated to Alex Brettin of Mild High Club, who then briefly appeared on stage. The Fourth Colour contained quotes of Gila Monster and Mars For the Rich. Straws In the Wind contained a Rattlesnake quote. Welcome to An Altered Future was played for the first time since 2018-03-16. (304 show gap) This was also the first time the suite was performed in full. Set featured Joey on guitar and Ambrose on saxophone. Head On/Pill was not on the written setlist and featured Ambrose on saxophone and contained Am I In Heaven? and Robot Stop teases, Chang'e teases and quotes, and a Hot Water jam with lyrics. At the end of the show, Ambrose thanked Chicago for The Blues Brothers and blues music.
venuename: Huntington Bank Pavilion at Northerly Island

shownotes:
Audience member Joe did the vocal intro to Nuclear Fusion. The Lord of Lightning contained an I'm In Your Mind tease. Cellophane contained Am I In Heaven? and Lord of Lightning teases. Mystery Jack was last played 2022-05-31. (138 show gap) Hypertension contained Witchcraft and Hot Water teases. Ambrose did a short breakdance after his verse of Le Risque and said Raygun taught him everything he knows. The Grim Reaper contained Telescope (Ambrose Kenny-Smith & GUM), Torture Chamber, Body Movin' and Intergalactic (Beastie Boys) quotes. Magenta Mountain was attempted but only a jam was played (Titled We're Doin' It Together on the Official Bootleg) because Joey's synth wasn't working. This Thing contained a The Land Before Timeland tease. The intro to Am I In Heaven? (which was not on the printed setlist) contained Lava and The Dripping Tap teases and the song itself contained a This Thing tease and a Hypertension jam with lyrics. 

Poster By Job van Rijen
Standard: 600
Gold Foil: 200

jamchart_notes: Transitioned into from Sad Pilot with a Rats in the Sky intro, this Magma is a bit heavier and messier than its counterparts from summer ‘24. A standout performance from Cavs particularly as his drumming steers the band heavier and heavier. The outro is taken to its usual heights, but as it falls out the band changes key to E and transitions smoothly into Rats in the Sky.

Amby joined opener Geese on 3D Country. Cellophane featured Ambrose on sax. Self-Immolate featured a drum solo with Riff Raff (AC/DC) teases from Stu ending in a Moby Dick (Led Zeppelin) tease. Motor Spirit was dedicated to everyone on the highway. Gilgamesh contained Gila Monster quotes. Set contained Gilgamesh quotes. Nuclear Fusion featured Hunter, an audience member, on the intro. Ataraxia, which was last played 2023-08-16 (54 show gap), had its lyrics changed to "I am a father now”. Field of Vision was dedicated to Jess Lewis (the band's tour manager who is mention in the song). Raw Feel contained a Sympathy For The Devil (The Rolling Stones) quote. Lava was last played 2023-06-03 (71 show gap) and did not contain the intro. Before Antarctica, Joey shouted out opener Geese and Stu was handed a fox mask, which he put on followed by a full band tease of Foxey Lady (Jimi Hendrix). Joey quoted Billy Madison as the song started “Extra Sloppy for ya.”

This show featured the debut of Hog Calling Contest. The first The Bitter Boogie contained the drums from Muddy Water and Muddy Water teases. Muddy Water contained the drums from The Bitter Boogie and The Bitter Boogie teases. The Bitter Boogie Reprise also contained a Muddy Water tease. Elbow was last played 2018-11-23. (257 show gap) After The River, which contained a Lost Woods (Koji Kondo) tease, the band wished Jay Weinberg a happy birthday. Le Risque and Gila Monster featured Jay Weinberg on drums. During Le Risque, Cavs went into the audience for his vocal part and asked an audience member (Devin) to do the "Let's Ride" lyric. Stressin’ was last played 2022-06-01. (141 show gap) Chang'e contained Witchcraft quotes. Extinction contained Gondii quotes.

Poster by Jason Galea

Anoxia was last played 2023-08-16. (57 show gap) The beginning of Billabong Valley contained Cotton-Eyed Joe (Traditional) quotes and an Automation tease. Tetrachromacy was last played 2022-10-14 (119 show gap). It's Got Old was last played 2023-06-04 (73 show gap). Before Mystery Jack, Zebra (John Butler) was teased. You Can Be Your Silhouette contained a Her and I (Slow Jam 2) tease. Dirt was last played 2022-04-13 (169 show gap), and contained a Hot Water tease. Following the Mirage City Reprise, the band teased and quoted We Will Rock You (Queen) with the lyrics changed to "We will Red Rock you". Field of Vision was dedicated to tour manager Jess Lewis. Tetrachromacy through Work This Time featured Joey on acoustic guitar. Rats In The Sky contained a Hot Water tease. Hot Water contained Nutbush City Limits (Ike & Tina Turner), Antarctica, Motor Spirit, and Iron Lung quotes, and Hell's Riff. Ambrose quoted If You Want My Love (Cheap Trick) as the band left the stage. Following the show those attending both the early and late shows were allowed to stay in the venue and Pipe-eye's upcoming album Pipe-defy and Heavy Moss' upcoming album Dead Slow were played over the PA.

Before Antarctica, which was introduced as a song about Antarctica and "Cookie's biggest regret thinking polar bears live [there]", Ambrose quoted the outro of Daily Blues. Ambergris was dedicated to sperm whales. Trapdoor was introduced as a song about "the dungeon Stu keeps Ambrose trapped in to make music". Before People-Vultures, Ambrose quoted Walk The Dinosaur (Was (Not Was)). People-Vultures itself contained Iron Lung quotes. Mr. Beat contained an Iron Lung jam with lyrics. jamchart_notes: A blisteringly fast and wet The Dripping Tap. An intense rain storm rolled in shortly before the band took the stage, concerning Ambrose in particular as he voiced his fear for his life throughout the performance. Out of the second chorus a familiar, yet faster than usual, jam emerges. The band wastes little time re-entering the song proper with a frenetic and energetic finish. Following the song, the show was halted for 22 minutes due to torrential downpour.
shownotes: This show took place during an unrelenting rain storm. The Dripping Tap concluded with the Streets of Cairo tease from I'm Not In Your Mind. Following Tap the band left the stage for approximately 22 minutes due to the dangers posed by wet equipment, which was repositioned to be out of the rain during the break. Boogieman Sam contained The Bitter Boogie teases. Amby quoted Singin' In the Rain (Arthur Freed/Nacio Herb Brown) in Daily Blues. Prior to Le Risque Joey shouted out Portugal. The Man. Following The Fourth Colour, Amby quoted Can't Keep My Eyes Off Of You (Frankie Valli) with lyrics changed to "I love you Portland" and John Gourley (of Portugal. The Man) came on stage and gave the band shots. Self-Immolate featured a drum solo with a Moby Dick (Led Zeppelin) tease. Supercell was introduced as a song about storms. The Lord of Lightning contained an extended outro with a Cellophane tease. The Balrog was last played 2023-03-20. (85 show gap) Poster By Jason Galea Standard: 600 Rainbow Foil: 200

footnote: Show resumed after 22 minute Rain Delay

The Lord of Lightning contained an extended outro with a Cellophane tease. The Balrog was last played 2023-03-20. (85 show gap) Poster By Jason Galea Standard: 600 Rainbow Foil: 200

This show took place during an unrelenting rain storm. The Dripping Tap concluded with the Streets of Cairo tease from I'm Not In Your Mind. Following Tap the band left the stage for approximately 22 minutes due to the dangers posed by wet equipment, which was repositioned to be out of the rain during the break. An audience member (Sheri) did the vocal intro to Nuclear Fusion. After the song, the crowd chanted Sheri’s name. Joey said "Free Palestine" in Pleura. During Straws In The Wind, Ambrose roamed the venue, eventually making it to the soundboard, where he played with the onstage visuals and exchanged banter with Stu and Joey. Before Mars For The Rich the band teased Moby Dick (Led Zeppelin). Following Raw Feel, Cavs teased Hey Pachuco (Royal Crown Revue) from The Mask. Garden Goblin was introduced as a dog based song Poster by Jason Galea

Rattlesnake contained Automation and Sleep Drifter teases, Boogieman Sam quotes and Nuclear Fusion teases. O.N.E. and Sleep Drifter featured Stu on microtonal acoustic guitar. Open Water contained a Chang’e tease. The Lord of Lightning contained an Ice V tease. The Balrog contained a Cellophane tease. Before Hog Calling Contest, Joey mentioned that the band saw Dom Dolla at The Gorge the night before. Joe then asked the crowd to pretend to call hogs, and then invited sound manager Joe Santarpia to do the same. Stu then picked up an Intrasport shirt from a fan and told them they weren't playing it at the show, but would next tour, followed be an Intrasport tease. Flight b741 contained a false start due to Amby playing in the wrong key. Ice V contained a Head On/Pill tease and a Down Under (Men At Work) tease on drums. Dirt through Let Me Mend The Past featured Joey on acoustic guitar. Dirt conained a “Taylor” (Jack Johnson) tease. Mirage City was introduced as Paradise City (Guns N' Roses) followed by a Welcome to the Jungle quote from Amby. During Let Me Mend The Past, Ambrose crowdsurfed on an inflatable boat. Following Le Risque, Joey shouted out Jackson Devereux and Allen Dobbins (the stream film crew). Gondii contained a Dragon quote. The Silver Cord contained Extinction quotes. Daily Blues was quoted before Venusian 1, which was stopped halfway through due to the barricade being broken. During the stop, Joey called his mom and Stu and Joey sung part of Vegemite acapella, which was "performed" for the first time since 2016-05-14. Gaia, which was incomplete, featured a drum solo with a Motor Spirit tease. The Great Chain of Being was incomplete. Gaia Outro was on the printed setlist after Motor Spirit, but was cut. Poster by Jason Galea Normal - 2500 Foil - 500

jamchart_notes: A stylish intro kicks off a funkier than usual Magma. A tight first verse and jam give way to playful springy flourishes in the bridge. An energetic and powerful second jam that strays from the usual course of Magma follows, with creative guitar lines from Stu and Joey dominating before settling back into the verse. The outro is taken to a familiar double time intensity, but again, unique guitar work from Stu and Joey lend it a distinct character and sound.

Mirage City was introduced as Paradise City (Guns N' Roses) followed by a Welcome to the Jungle quote from Amby. During Let Me Mend The Past, Ambrose crowdsurfed on an inflatable boat. Following Le Risque, Joey shouted out Jackson Devereux and Allen Dobbins (the stream film crew). Gondii contained a Dragon quote. The Silver Cord contained Extinction quotes. Daily Blues was quoted before Venusian 1, which was stopped halfway through due to the barricade being broken. During the stop, Joey called his mom and Stu and Joey sung part of Vegemite acapella, which was "performed" for the first time since 2016-05-14. Gaia, which was incomplete, featured a drum solo with a Motor Spirit tease. The Great Chain of Being was incomplete. Gaia Outro was on the printed setlist after Motor Spirit, but was cut. Poster by Jason Galea Normal - 2500 Foil - 500

Dirt through Let Me Mend The Past featured Joey on acoustic guitar. Dirt conained a “Taylor” (Jack Johnson) tease. Mirage City was introduced as Paradise City (Guns N' Roses) followed by a Welcome to the Jungle quote from Amby. During Let Me Mend The Past, Ambrose crowdsurfed on an inflatable boat. Following Le Risque, Joey shouted out Jackson Devereux and Allen Dobbins (the stream film crew). Gondii contained a Dragon quote. The Silver Cord contained Extinction quotes. Daily Blues was quoted before Venusian 1, which was stopped halfway through due to the barricade being broken. During the stop, Joey called his mom and Stu and Joey sung part of Vegemite acapella, which was "performed" for the first time since 2016-05-14. Gaia, which was incomplete, featured a drum solo with a Motor Spirit tease. The Great Chain of Being was incomplete. Gaia Outro was on the printed setlist after Motor Spirit, but was cut. Poster by Jason Galea Normal - 2500 Foil - 500

Slow Jam 1 contained Crumbling Castle and Am I In Heaven? teases. This Thing contained Am I In Heaven? teases and quotes. Am I In Heaven? contained The Land Before Timeland, Whole Lotta Rosie (AC/DC) and Witchcraft teases and a Slow Jam 1 jam with lyrics, and featured improvised alternate lyrics. Self-Immolate featured a drum solo by Cavs. Nuclear Fusion featured community taper Mystery Jack for the vocal intro. Hog Calling Contest was teased prior to Antarctica, which contained Am I In Heaven? teases. Boogieman Sam contained La Grange (ZZ Top) teases and a Riff Raff (AC/DC) jam. Following Boogieman Sam, Joey mentioned that the band would be performing at the Hollywood Bowl with the L.A. Philharmonic on their upcoming tour in 2025, which was previously unannounced at the time. Texas Flood and Pride and Joy (Stevie Ray Vaughan) were teased prior to Daily Blues, which contained Hot Water teases. Robot Stop contained Hot Water teases, This Thing quotes, Forty Six and 2 (Tool) teases and Master of the Universe (Hawkwind) teases and quotes. Gamma Knife was unfinished. Ice V contained The Lord of Lightning teases. The Lord of Lightning contained Cellophane teases. Le Risque featured Jay Weinberg on drums. Additionally, Cavs got an audience member to perform a laugh for the song and brought Jay's wife Chloe on stage to perform the 'Let's Ride' vocal part. Following Le Risque, Amby quoted I Love L.A. (Randy Newman).

Chang'e contained Witchcraft quotes from the extended mix. Sense contained Witchcraft teases. Following Raw Feel, Cavs teased Hey Pachuco (Royal Crown Revue) from The Mask. Ambrose teased Flight b741 before Dirt. Following Dirt, Ambrose called Joey "Joe Dirt". Following Flight b741, Joey asked the crowd if anyone in the band had been to their 2022 show in San Diego (2022-04-11) and Stu asked if anyone had been to The Soda Bar (2016-05-18 and 2015-08-25), mentioning there was someone dressed in a panda suit there, who he then spotted in the crowd. Iron Lung contained Can't Stop (Red Hot Chili Peppers) and Witchcraft teases. Muddy Water contained The Bitter Boogie and The Castle In The Air teases. The Bitter Boogie contained the drums from Muddy Water as well as teases. After The Bitter Boogie, Joey teased Good Riddance (Green Day) and Every Morning (Sugar Ray) and asked Sammy to turn his mic through the PA, and Joey teased Every Morning and Good Riddance again. Ambrose then jokingly brought up Green Day’s infamous 2012 iHeartRadio incident (during which the band destroyed their instruments angrily after being told their set was being cut short) and multiple band members quoted Billie Joe Armstrong’s exclamations from the performance. God Is Calling Me Back Home had lyrics changed to "She's calling me back home" and featured Joey playing guitar with a beer bottle. Inbetween God Is Calling Me Back Home and Magma, Joey teased Tell Me Why (Neil Young). Magma was played for Sammy, featured an extended intro where the band shouted out their crew members, did not have the "rumble" verse, and contained a The Silver Cord jam with lyrics. After Magma, the band jokingly played a portion of Gaia acoustically. Theia contained Motor Spirit teases & quotes and The River teases. Only Part I of The River (which was introduced as a song about "when you wake up in the morning, and you smell something, in the kitchen, it’s not supposed to be there, and it smells real good, and you go eat it, and then you feel real good, then you feel real sick") was performed and it contained Motor Spirit teases, Theia teases, and a The Silver Cord vocal jam with Theia quotes. Honey and Pleura were on some of the written setlists following Horology but were replaced with Magma.
venuename: The Rady Shell at Jacobs Park

Honey and Pleura were on some of the written setlists following Horology but were replaced with Magma.
venuename: The Rady Shell at Jacobs Park

jamchart_notes: Acoustic. Riding a steady disco drumbeat throughout, opening with extended banter where the band lists off their crew. Unlike the heavy vibe of traditional Magmas, it flows into a lighter, psychedelic Silver Cord jam with lyrics.

shownotes: This show featured the debut of Intrasport, which featured Lucas on Joey's microtonal guitar and Joey on tambourine. Following Billabong Valley Joey shouted out King Stingray and their relationship to Yothu Yindi and Warumpi Band. Hypertension contained teases of Whole Lotta Rosie (AC/DC), La Grange (ZZ Top) and Ice V. Stu teased La Grange again before Antarctica. Joey shouted out Cookie's new Pipe-Eye album in the intro to Ambergris. A segue from Sad Pilot into Rats In the Sky was completed; immediately following the segue the show was stopped for approximately 4 minutes to help a fan who passed out and Rats was then restarted from the beginning. Gaia featured a drum solo by Cavs.

Hypertension contained teases of Whole Lotta Rosie (AC/DC), La Grange (ZZ Top) and Ice V. Stu teased La Grange again before Antarctica. Joey shouted out Cookie's new Pipe-Eye album in the intro to Ambergris. A segue from Sad Pilot into Rats In the Sky was completed; immediately following the segue the show was stopped for approximately 4 minutes to help a fan who passed out and Rats was then restarted from the beginning. Gaia featured a drum solo by Cavs.

This show featured the debut of Interior People, which featured Joey on acoustic guitar. I’m In Your Mind and I'm Not In Your Mind contained Cellophane teases. During I’m Not In Your Mind, Joey shouted out a young fan on his parent’s shoulders. Cut Throat Boogie contained Sad Pilot, Daily Blues and Riff Raff (AC/DC) teases. Trapdoor contained a Forty Six and 2 (Tool) tease. Before Hog Calling Contest, Joey mentioned how Ambrose invented a skate move called "Chicken to the Barnyard" and how he played Skate. During the banter, Stu put on clothes thrown in from the crowd, Joey asked who was going to the Rave Matthews Band show on Wednesday and teased “Taylor” (Jack Johnson) at which point Stu introduced the song as being by Jack Johnson. The song itself featured Joey on acoustic guitar and a pig squeal by audio tech Joe “Santo” Santarpia. Field of Vision was introduced as a song about the audience and was dedicated to Jess Lewis. Raw Feel contained Robot Stop, Witchcraft, and Third Stone From The Sun (Jimi Hendrix) teases. After the song, Joey complimented the venue, calling it “forest Red Rocks.” Ambrose quoted Le Risque before The Dripping Tap, which was dedicated to Billy Cavanaugh and contained Daily Blues, Cellophane and Magma teases and Magma quotes. Lucas referenced Pleura at the end of the show.

Bullant set after King Gizzard's show that same night. Stu came on stage during Shattering the Norm (Obstructor). Ambrose came on during Graveyard Shift (Ned Bennett) and stayed for the next song, quoting My Neck, My Back (Lick It) (Khia). During K On My D + C (Partiboi69, Juicy Romance) Joey gave a shout out to Partiboi69 and sang along. Joey also gave a shout out during My City. Ambrose, Cavs, Cook and Lucas joined for Kraken (Stef Mendesidis) while Ambrose stayed for the next song, quoting Le Risque and Intergalactic (Beastie Boys). Ambrose stayed on stage for the following songs including Pump Up the Jam (Technotronic), LIKE A PUNK (Joey Valence & Brae) and B.O.B. (Bombs Over Baghdad) before leaving for Breathe (The Prodigy). Ambrose returned with Gaspard de Meulemeester for Techno Disco Tool (Mella Dee) and quoted We Are Family (Sister Sledge).

jamchart_notes: Fantastic composed section that flows into a "Theia" vocal sample jam, followed by a synth pattern that almost feels like percussion layered over Cavs' drumming into the "Motor Spirit" callback, leading into another percussive synth section with robotic bleep-like noises, crashing into a high-energy finale before calming down -> The Silver Cord.
shownotes: This show featured a performance of The Silver Cord in full. Theia contained Motor Spirit quotes from the extended mix. Set contained Pump Up the Jam (Technotronic) quotes and the Converge quotes from the extended mix. Chang'e contained the Witchcraft quotes from the extended mix. Gilgamesh contained the Gila Monster quotes from the extended mix. Swan Song contained In Da Club (50 Cent) quotes, the Dragon quotes from the extended mix and a quote of That's the Way (I Like It) (KC and the Sunshine Band). Extinction contained an Incredible (M Beat) quote, a Shanghai tease, and the Flamethrower quotes from the extended mix. Shanghai was also teased directly following the end of Extinction. Intrasport contained a Gonna Make You Sweat (Everybody Dance Now) (C+C Music Factory) quote. Shanghai and The Grim Reaper were on the setlist, before and after Intrasport respectively, but were not played.

jamchart_notes: Transitioning from Theia, this rendition settles into an intro jam with Stu singing "The Silver Cord" through a vocoder, accompanied by orchestral samples. This leads into an ethereal composed section, followed by a darker, more intense passage with vocal samples layered over strong drumbeats from Cavs. The music then shifts into an entirely different sound that feels like alien dance music, with static-like synths at moments, ending in an ambient section where percussive drumming fades into Set.
shownotes: This show featured a performance of The Silver Cord in full. Theia contained Motor Spirit quotes from the extended mix. Swan Song contained In Da Club (50 Cent) quotes, the Dragon quotes from the extended mix and a quote of That's the Way (I Like It) (KC and the Sunshine Band). Shanghai and The Grim Reaper were on the setlist, before and after Intrasport respectively, but were not played.

jamchart_notes: -> The Silver Cord, the bassline fades in alongside chipmunk-like vocal samples. The bass fades out, then returns with a strong drop into the composed section featuring a disco drumbeat. After a fiery composed part, with Ambrose adding quotes from Pump Up the Jam, they build to a huge drop followed by various synth textures. Joey then switches to guitar, Lucas on bass, and the funk kicks in. They then ease into an ambient funk section with bass bombs from Lucas, before one last drop and -> Chang'e.

Shanghai and The Grim Reaper were on the setlist, before and after Intrasport respectively, but were not played.

This show featured a performance of The Silver Cord in full. Theia contained Motor Spirit quotes from the extended mix. Set contained Pump Up the Jam (Technotronic) quotes and the Converge quotes from the extended mix. Chang'e contained the Witchcraft quotes from the extended mix. Gilgamesh contained the Gila Monster quotes from the extended mix. Swan Song contained In Da Club (50 Cent) quotes, the Dragon quotes from the extended mix and a quote of That's the Way (I Like It) (KC and the Sunshine Band). Extinction contained an Incredible (M Beat) quote, a Shanghai tease, and the Flamethrower quotes from the extended mix. Shanghai was also teased directly following the end of Extinction. Intrasport contained a Gonna Make You Sweat (Everybody Dance Now) (C+C Music Factory) quote. Shanghai and The Grim Reaper were on the setlist, before and after Intrasport respectively, but were not played.

Evil Death Roll contained Iron Lung teases and quotes. Sad Pilot contained Hot Water teases. Rats In the Sky was dedicated to Elvis Presley. During Daily Blues, Ambrose quoted I Feel Like a Woman (Shania Twain) while wearing a cowboy hat from her Vegas residency. Shanghai contained vocal samples from a slot machine, Magenta Mountain teases, and Grow Wings and Fly quotes. The Grim Reaper also contained samples from a slot machine, chants of "cashing the check" and "Vegas", as well as Money Maker (Ludacris), Viva Las Vegas (Elvis Presley), and Intergalactic (Beastie Boys) quotes. Perihelion was introduced as a new song called Break Stuff, then Ambrose quoted the song by Limp Bizkit. Dragon contained The Great Chain Of Being teases.  Billabong Valley was teased prior to Rattlesnake, which contained Nuclear Fusion and Sleep Drifter teases.

Gaia featured a drum solo by Cavs. This Thing contained an Incredible (M Beat) quote. Crumbling Castle contained a Loyalty tease. The Fourth Colour contained teases of Whole Lotta Rosie (AC/DC) and Forty Six and 2 (Tool).  Nuclear Fusion's intro was sung by an audience member named Rob. Prior to Intrasport, Ambrose and Stu gave Lucas a shoutout for playing guitar on the track and Ambrose introduced a gong solo from Cavs. Set contained an Ice V tease. Ambrose introduced Antarctica as a song Cook wrote about his love of air conditioning. Joey tried to imitate Jacob Collier by instructing the audience to do throat singing before Black Tooth. Iron Lung contained People-Vultures teases.  Le Risque was introduced as a song about a little grey mouse and contained a Whole Lotta Rosie (AC/DC) tease. Boogieman Sam was preceded by teases of Riff Raff (AC/DC), Moby Dick (Led Zeppelin) and Whole Lotta Rosie (AC/DC), and contained a Whole Lotta Rosie (AC/DC) jam with Field of Vision quotes and Riff Raff (AC/DC) teases. This led to a People-Vultures jam with Muddy Water teases and Iron Lung and Phantom Island quotes.  Mirage City was on the written setlist after Antarctica but was not played.

Mirage City was introduced as a "country song," featured Stu on acoustic guitar and contained a jam with lyrics of I’ll Be Gone (Spectrum) and a Riff Raff (AC/DC) tease. Raw Feel also featured Stu on acoustic guitar. Ice V contained Hog Calling Contest teases. Ambrose quoted the "one million dollars" line from Austin Powers and "Scotty Doesn't Know" by Lustra in the intro to Magma. The Dripping Tap was introduced as being sponsored by Pepto Bismol and contained You Can Be Your Silhouette and Machine Gun (Jimi Hendrix) teases. Work This Time was on the written setlist after Raw Feel but was not played.

Plastic Boogie contained Hypertension, The Land Before Timeland, The Dripping Tap, and This Thing teases. Straws In The Wind contained I’m Bored (Iggy Pop), Smoke & Mirrors, and Phantom Island quotes. Before Intrasport, the band introduced the synth table with its new nickname "Nathan". Set contained Ice V and Work This Time teases. Extinction contained the Flamethrower quotes from the extended mix. Irong Lung contained Hypertension teases. Hypertension contained a Crumbling Castle tease and a The Dripping Tap tease. Hog Calling Contest was teased before Antarctica, which contained Hot Water teases. Hog Calling Contest and Flight b741 featured Joey on acoustic guitar. The River contained Wah Wah teases and Row, Row, Row Your Boat (traditional) quotes.  Le Risque was on the written setlist after The River but was not played.

Hog Calling Contest and Flight b741 featured Joey on acoustic guitar. The River contained Wah Wah teases and Row, Row, Row Your Boat (traditional) quotes.  Le Risque was on the written setlist after The River but was not played.

Hot Water contained Daily Blues teases and a Robot Stop snippet as the outro. Gamma Knife contained a Motor Spirit jam with lyrics. People-Vultures contained Iron Lung teases. Sad Pilot contained Daily Blues teases. Rats In The Sky contained an ad-libbed line “got myself some chicken wings.”The Bitter Boogie contained many Muddy Water teases in the intro, as well as the drums from Muddy Water, and had the first 2 verses swapped. Muddy Water had the drums from The Bitter Boogie. Mirage City contained an intro jam and an alternate slower outro. The Grim Reaper contained a Get Low (Lil Jon) quote with the lyrics changed to "To the window, To Arkansas" and a Hog Calling Contest quote. Magenta Mountain contained an extended intro. Flamethrower was introduced as a song about a dragon setting the world on fire.

This show featured the debut of both Grow Wings and Fly and Treaty (Yothu Yindi). Head On/Pill contained The Land Before Timeland, Chang’e, Am I In Heaven? and Sad Pilot teases and Grow Wings and Fly quotes. Following Head On/Pill, Stu and Joey mentioned that the first show they played outside of Australia was in Austin 10 years ago (2014-5-2). Wah Wah contained an intro, quote, and jam of The River, as well as Ice V teases. Before Nuclear Fusion, Joey noted that Cavs was a big fan of Ferrari so he was happy about them playing inside an F1 track, then talked about how Cavs won a go kart race before the show and announced that it was Mystery Jack's 100th King Gizzard show. A fan, Ethan, was brought up to do the vocal intro to Nuclear Fusion, and he quoted Straws In The Wind before the line. Straws In The Wind contained Phantom Island quotes. Set contained an Ain't It Funky Now (James Brown) tease. During Shanghai Cavs hit his gong and it cracked. Mars For The Rich contained a false start due to the band starting without Lucas. During Treaty, the band were joined by Stuart Kellaway of Yothu Yindi on bass and all of the members of King Stingray. Her and I (Slow Jam 2) was on the printed setlist after Le Risque but was cut. Joey joined opener King Stingray on a cover of Waru (Fire) by Warumpi Band.

During Treaty, the band were joined by Stuart Kellaway of Yothu Yindi on bass and all of the members of King Stingray. Her and I (Slow Jam 2) was on the printed setlist after Le Risque but was cut. Joey joined opener King Stingray on a cover of Waru (Fire) by Warumpi Band.

This show featured the debut of both Grow Wings and Fly and Treaty (Yothu Yindi). Head On/Pill contained The Land Before Timeland, Chang’e, Am I In Heaven? and Sad Pilot teases and Grow Wings and Fly quotes. Following Head On/Pill, Stu and Joey mentioned that the first show they played outside of Australia was in Austin 10 years ago (2014-5-2). Wah Wah contained an intro, quote, and jam of The River, as well as Ice V teases. footnote: Debut; with King Stingray and Stuart Kellaway
footnotes: ["Debut; with King Stingray and Stuart Kellaway"]
jamchart_notes: Similar in groove and funkiness to the recent Set synth table jams, the band is joined by opening act King Stingray to perform a funky cover of the Yothu Yindi song about Indigenous Australian’s campaign for recognition as traditional owners of the land, accompanied by founding bass guitarist Stuart Kellaway

Joey introduced Cavs as "Michael Caviar" before Hell and after Self-Immolate.  This Thing contained quotes of She's So Fine (The Jimi Hendrix Experience) and Incredible (M Beat). The Grim Reaper contained Get Low (Lil John), Torture Chamber, and Extinction quotes. Extinction contained the Flamethrower quotes from the extended mix. Robot Stop contained Hot Water teases. Gamma Knife contained a People-Vultures outro.

showtitle: The SONGS FOR KIDS "YOUNG" SERIES: EPISODE 4
shownotes:
A collaborative pre-show performance of Flight B741 with members of The Songs For Kids Band.

The Songs For Kids Band is an ensemble of kids and young adults with disabilities and illnesses from the Songs For Kids music mentorship program. The band emphasizes the power of teamwork and support for each other, bringing together kids that would otherwise be on a more solitary journey.

Songs For Kids is a 501(c)3 non profit that provides music mentorship and interactive programs for kids and young adults with illnesses, injuries, and disabilities. Focused on creative expression, encourages exploration in a safe space, and never lose sight of the fun.

Her and I contained Ice V and Slow Jam 1 teases and Iron Lung teases and quotes. Before Crumbling Castle, Ambrose thanked the crowd and thanked the city for OutKast. Hog Calling Contest and Moby Dick (Led Zeppelin) were teased before Flight b741. Iron Lung contained a Lava intro. Between Sad Pilot and Rats in the Sky, Ambrose shouted out the previous venues the band played in Atlanta, and made an Austin Powers reference. The Garden Goblin was dedicated to cameraman Allen and his partner who requested the song.  Following Gilgamesh Amby quoted Time After Time (Cyndi Lauper).  Set was on the written setlist as the final song but was not played.
